title: Hide secrets from container inspect command
body:
Secret mounted as an env variables are exposed by the `podman container inspect` command.

This change replaces the content of the env var by `*******`.

To reproduce this, follow the commands reported by the original issue:
```bash
printf 'secret value' | podman secret create mysecret -
podman container run --rm --secret mysecret,type=env -d alpine sleep 300
podman container inspect -l --format '{{ .Config.Env }}'
```

Fixes #23788

```release-note
Fixed secrets being exposed by `podman container inspect` when mounted as an env var
```

I will squash the commits. 
I was unable to run the e2e tests in my machines (and found no assistance in Matrix or irc). So I created the draft PR to at least see the test results (I'm not too familiar with Ginkgo).

>  I was unable to run the e2e tests in my machines (and found no assistance in Matrix or irc). So I created the draft PR to at least see the test results (I'm not too familiar with Ginkgo).

What is the error/problem? 

Try
```
make localintegration FOCUS="podman inspect hides secrets mounted to env"
```
to run only your test, the first time it will take a while as it pull all our test images in setup.

> I am not sure if this breaks anyone but I agree it seems reasonable to have the env value hidden like this by default. @mheon @ashley-cui opinions?

Probably a good idea, thanks!
I'm in favor, seems incorrect to be displaying these in inspect output.
I agree. `podman secret inspect` hides it by default as well, and has a --showsecret option.

Thanks for the input. I applied the changes and fixes my tests. All commits squashed into one.
Let me know if I should make other changes (maybe move this elsewhere?), or if can help with anything else.

---
About running e2e locally: 
> What is the error/problem?


I was unable to run the tests in my Arch system. But I booted a Fedora machine and it worked fine.

In Arch, I saw this error:
```
configure.ac:34: error: AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE expanded multiple times
/usr/share/aclocal-1.17/init.m4:29: AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE is expanded from...
configure.ac:19: the top level
/usr/share/aclocal-1.17/init.m4:29: AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE is expanded from...
configure.ac:34: the top level
autom4te: error: /usr/bin/m4 failed with exit status: 1
aclocal: error: /usr/bin/autom4te failed with exit status: 1
autoreconf: error: aclocal failed with exit status: 1
```
If I track it down, I'll open an issue/PR about it.

---

I cannot make any sense of the error unfortunately, Podman is only using a simple makefile and not any of the configure/automake/autoconf stuff so I wonder what is running there.
